claim: Agents are autonomous systems that manage their own context and trajectory, distinct from simple workflows where you define inputs/outputs or single LM features.
actionable: When designing a system, classify it as agent (autonomous, self-directing) vs workflow (structured steps) vs single LM feature (categorization/completion), then choose architecture accordingly.
source_title: Claude Agent SDK [Full Workshop] — Thariq Shihipar, Anthropic

claim: The bash tool is the most powerful agent tool because it enables composability, reuse of existing software (FFmpeg, grep, awk), dynamic script generation, and state management through the file system.
actionable: Prioritize bash as your primary tool in agents over custom tools; use it for composing APIs, searching data, and storing results in files for context.
source_title: Claude Agent SDK [Full Workshop] — Thariq Shihipar, Anthropic

claim: Agents operate in a loop of three steps: gather context, take action, verify work. Agents with stronger verification steps (like compilation/linting for code) are more reliable than those without.
actionable: Design agent workflows around this three-step loop; prioritize verifiable domains (code via linting, databases via queries returning results) over less verifiable ones (deep research with only citations).
source_title: Claude Agent SDK [Full Workshop] — Thariq Shihipar, Anthropic

claim: Use tools for atomic, high-control actions (writing files, sending emails, reversible changes); bash for composable searches and data processing; code generation for dynamic, flexible logic.
actionable: When designing an agent's action layer, map each capability to the right tool type: tools for user-facing reversible actions, bash for composing existing utilities, code gen for complex dynamic logic.
source_title: Claude Agent SDK [Full Workshop] — Thariq Shihipar, Anthropic

claim: Gathering context is often under-thought; creative iteration on search interfaces (CSV queries via SQLite, spreadsheet formulas like B3:B5, XML queries) often yields better agent performance than a single approach.
actionable: When building agents for data retrieval, try multiple search interface designs (SQL, regex, range notation); test with a few sample queries and let the agent guide which it prefers.
source_title: Claude Agent SDK [Full Workshop] — Thariq Shihipar, Anthropic

claim: Sub-agents are critical for managing context explosion; use them for compute-heavy searches, data processing, or verification tasks that don't need to pollute the main agent's context.
actionable: When an agent's context approaches limits, spin off sub-agents for isolated subtasks (e.g., searching different spreadsheet sheets in parallel) to isolate and summarize results.
source_title: Claude Agent SDK [Full Workshop] — Thariq Shihipar, Anthropic

claim: Verification should happen at multiple points in the agent loop, not just at the end; use deterministic rules (e.g., row count limits, file read before write) and feedback mechanisms to guide agents.
actionable: Implement verification hooks at multiple stages: after context gathering (check data format), during action (enforce preconditions), and at end (final validation); give agents error feedback to iterate.
source_title: Claude Agent SDK [Full Workshop] — Thariq Shihipar, Anthropic

claim: Context engineering through the file system is more powerful than via prompts alone; store long outputs, intermediate results, and memory in files so agents can search and reference them.
actionable: Design agents to store tool outputs, intermediate calculations, and memory in the file system (e.g., results.json, scratch_notes.md) so agents can grep, search, and cross-reference across runs.
source_title: Claude Agent SDK [Full Workshop] — Thariq Shihipar, Anthropic

claim: Security in agents uses a Swiss cheese defense: model alignment, harness-level permissioning (AST parsing for bash, tool validation), and sandboxing (network/filesystem isolation).
actionable: When deploying agents, implement multi-layer security: align model behavior, add harness-level permission checks, and sandbox network/filesystem access. Never rely on a single layer.
source_title: Claude Agent SDK [Full Workshop] — Thariq Shihipar, Anthropic

claim: Skills are a form of progressive context disclosure for out-of-distribution tasks; they contain detailed prompts and file structures that agents discover via the file system and bash.
actionable: For complex, specialized tasks (e.g., front-end design, document generation), create a skill as a folder with detailed claude.md + supporting files; agents will cd into it and discover instructions.
source_title: Claude Agent SDK [Full Workshop] — Thariq Shihipar, Anthropic

claim: Agent code generation should iterate based on model feedback; code needs linting, testing, and error feedback loops. Throw errors when preconditions aren't met to guide iteration.
actionable: When designing agent code generation, include linting steps, test execution, and error messages that guide the agent to fix mistakes. Let agents iterate on feedback.
source_title: Claude Agent SDK [Full Workshop] — Thariq Shihipar, Anthropic

claim: Reversibility matters: agents are good at reversible domains (code with git history, spreadsheets with checkpoints) but struggle with irreversible state (e.g., computer use). Design reversible workflows when possible.
actionable: Before building an agent for a domain, assess reversibility: can mistakes be undone? Code=reversible (git), spreadsheets=reversible (snapshots), UI=not reversible. Build reversibility in.
source_title: Claude Agent SDK [Full Workshop] — Thariq Shihipar, Anthropic

claim: For large data (millions of rows/columns), don't load all into context at once; design agents to navigate progressively (like humans), using scratch pads and searching incrementally.
actionable: For large datasets, teach agents to search incrementally (start with headers, grep for matches, navigate sheets), maintain scratch pads for notes, and avoid loading entire datasets into context.
source_title: Claude Agent SDK [Full Workshop] — Thariq Shihipar, Anthropic

claim: Clear context frequently rather than waiting for compaction; if state is in files/git, agents can continue tasks without entire chat history.
actionable: When using agents, periodically clear the conversation and reset context. Ask the agent to reference outstanding changes (git diff, open tasks) rather than keeping full chat history.
source_title: Claude Agent SDK [Full Workshop] — Thariq Shihipar, Anthropic

claim: API design for agents is critical; choose interfaces that agents already understand (SQL for databases, bash/awk for data, XML/JSON for structured data, spreadsheet ranges).
actionable: When designing agent APIs, consider in-distribution knowledge: expose SQL queries for databases, bash commands for tools, and leverage spreadsheet syntax agents already know.
source_title: Claude Agent SDK [Full Workshop] — Thariq Shihipar, Anthropic

claim: Code generation for non-coding tasks (weather queries, data analysis, document generation) is powerful; agents compose APIs dynamically rather than using static tools.
actionable: For tasks needing dynamic data fetching or API composition (weather + clothing suggestions, data queries + analysis), use code generation rather than fixed tools.
source_title: Claude Agent SDK [Full Workshop] — Thariq Shihipar, Anthropic

claim: Prototype agents in Claude Code first to understand the domain, then productionize via Agent SDK hosted in sandboxes (Cloudflare, Modal, AWS) or locally.
actionable: Build agents iteratively: prototype in Claude Code to test context/action/verification loops, then port to Agent SDK + file system + hooks for production deployment.
source_title: Claude Agent SDK [Full Workshop] — Thariq Shihipar, Anthropic

claim: For custom agent communication and data sharing between parallel agents, use existing primitives (HTTP, bash, API keys, named pipes) rather than inventing new frameworks.
actionable: When multiple agents need to coordinate, have them use HTTP requests, bash pipes, or shared file system rather than building custom message queues.
source_title: Claude Agent SDK [Full Workshop] — Thariq Shihipar, Anthropic

claim: Hooks enable deterministic verification and context injection; use them to enforce preconditions (e.g., read before write), insert live context updates, or trigger on events.
actionable: Use Agent SDK hooks to verify preconditions after each tool call (e.g., deny writes to unread files) and to insert context updates from external sources (user changes to shared data).
source_title: Claude Agent SDK [Full Workshop] — Thariq Shihipar, Anthropic

claim: Tools should have high context usage; prefer bash for multiple tool composition; code gen for dynamic logic. Balance structural reliability (tools) with flexibility (bash/code gen).
actionable: When choosing between tools, bash, and code gen: use tools only for atomic, user-facing actions; bash for composing utilities; code gen for flexibility requiring iteration.
source_title: Claude Agent SDK [Full Workshop] — Thariq Shihipar, Anthropic

claim: For databases, grant agents broad access with guardrails rather than restrictive APIs; limit write scope and provide feedback on forbidden operations.
actionable: When connecting agents to databases, enable dynamic SQL queries and broad read access; enforce write limits and role-based access via API keys; give agents errors on forbidden operations.
source_title: Claude Agent SDK [Full Workshop] — Thariq Shihipar, Anthropic

claim: Agent monetization should be usage-based or subscription; focus on solving hard problems users pay for, and design rate limits/pricing into the system from the start.
actionable: When building agent products, solve expensive problems (code review, data analysis, software reliability) and charge based on usage or subscription; avoid changing pricing later.
source_title: Claude Agent SDK [Full Workshop] — Thariq Shihipar, Anthropic

claim: Re-evaluate agent assumptions every 6 months as model capabilities improve; avoid hedging bets on where AI goes; focus on what works today and be ready to rewrite.
actionable: When building agents, assume code has a 6-month shelf life due to model improvements; build fast, validate with real users, and be willing to refactor as capabilities evolve.
source_title: Claude Agent SDK [Full Workshop] — Thariq Shihipar, Anthropic
